quotes: Kindly prepare to wait efficiently for the inevitable disappointment.
Please stand by with urgency while nothing improves.
Wait quickly and carefully for things to go exactly as expected.
Remain on standby while we precisely deliver disappointment.
Please hurry up and wait for the outcome you wont enjoy.
Stand ready for disappointment, delivered right on schedule.
Please hold with purpose while things fail as planned.
Prepare immediately for delays and dissatisfaction.
Wait attentively while expectations are systematically lowered.
Please remain efficient during this extended period of letdown.
Act quickly to position yourself for maximum disappointment.
Please expedite your waiting for optimal frustration.
Stand by with confidence while everything unravels.
Prepare thoroughly for the disappointment weve been refining.
Wait with intention as we carefully orchestrate failure.
We ask that you remain agile while disappointment is deployed.
Please align your expectations with our upcoming underperformance.
Stand by for a timely delivery of unmet expectations.
Kindly maintain readiness while results fall short of projections.
We appreciate your patience as we execute this disappointment.
Dont expect the unexpected, expect stupid and you will rarely be surprised.
The unexpected is rare. Stupid is reliable. Plan accordingly.
You cant predict everything, but you can always predict stupid.
Hope for the best, expect the worst, and count on stupid.
The unexpected keeps you guessing but expecting stupid keeps you prepared.
Expecting the unexpected is optimistic. Expecting stupid is practical.
You wont be ready for everything but expecting stupid is a safe bet.
The unexpected happens occasionally. Stupid shows up daily.
If you prepare for stupid, everything else feels like a bonus.
If its not unnecessary, its at least aggressively pointless.
If its not superfluous, its definitely in the mouse-milking category.
If it adds value, its hiding it extremely well.
If its useful, its purely by accident.
If its not redundant, its working very hard to appear that way.
If its essential, weve done an excellent job disguising it.
If its not pointless, its at least point-adjacent.
If theres a purpose, its being kept confidential.
If its not wasted effort, its an impressive imitation.
If it matters, no one has informed the process.
If its not unnecessary, its unnecessarily complicated enough to qualify.
If its not mouse milking, its at least rodent-adjacent labor.
If its not pointless, its going out of its way to seem that way.
If theres value here, its microscopic and hard to extract.
If its not completely useless, its putting in a strong effort.
If its not superfluous, its a highly refined non-value-added activity.
If its not redundant, its strategically unnecessary.
If its not waste, its pre-waste.
If its not inefficiency, its efficiency in disguise.
If its not unnecessary, its awaiting justification.
Be indispensable and you will never go anywhere.
The better you are at your job, the less likely you are to escape it.
Excel at your role and youll be permanently assigned to it.
If removing you breaks everything, theyll just leave you there forever.
The more essential you are, the less mobile you become.
Irreplaceable is just another word for permanently assigned.
We value redundancy in roles, not in people.
Career growth requires operational expendability.
We encourage development as long as it doesnt create issues.
Management is where we relocate the problem so it becomes a strategy.
Those who cannot do are reassigned to oversee those who can.
We move inefficiency up the ladder where it becomes less noticeable.
The path to management is paved with consistently low expectations.
When performance is lacking we expand the scope and call it leadership.
Our promotion strategy ensures the least capable are supervising the most capable.
We do not eliminate inefficiency we reorganize it into management.
If you cannot contribute effectively you may qualify to manage those who can.
The less useful you are the higher we place you for maximum containment.
Leadership is simply failure with better seating.
We isolate underperformance in offices with titles.
Ineffectiveness rises until it reaches a level where it can no longer be measured.
Management is where confusion goes to give directions.
We align talent by moving low performance into high visibility roles.
Promotion is our primary tool for redistributing inefficiency.
We do not reduce risk we elevate it into leadership.
Underperformance is best managed by giving it authority.
We optimize outcomes by separating execution from decision making.
